pattern: Rank elements by sorting their indices on value, then map the top three placements to medal labels and the rest to their position number.
hints: You need each athlete's placement, which is its position after sorting all scores from highest to lowest.
Sort the indices by their score descending so the first index in that order is rank one, the second is rank two, and so on.
Walk the sorted indices and assign Gold, Silver, Bronze to the first three, then the placement number as a string to the remaining ones, writing each back to its original index.

edge_inputs.description: exactly three athletes fill all medals
ascending order so the last index is gold
two athletes only need gold and silver

stress.description: random permutation of distinct scores so all values are unique
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randrange(1, size + 1)
    vals = rng.sample(range(0, 1000001), n)
    return [vals]
